steps.description: Verifies that Purlin instruction files are internally consistent with each other and with the framework's README. Purlin-specific: consumer projects do not own instruction files.
steps.agent_instructions: Cross-reference `instructions/HOW_WE_WORK_BASE.md`, `instructions/ARCHITECT_BASE.md`, `instructions/BUILDER_BASE.md`, `instructions/QA_BASE.md`, and `features/policy_critic.md`. Check for: direct contradictions between files, stale file path references, terminology mismatches, and lifecycle/protocol definitions that differ between the shared philosophy and role-specific instructions. Also verify that README.md is consistent with the current instruction file content. Fix any inconsistencies and commit.

steps.description: Cross-references all Critic-related specification and instruction files for inconsistencies in terminology, routing rules, and responsibilities. Halts the release if critical inconsistencies are found. Once clean, writes or updates the '## The Critic' section in README.md.
steps.agent_instructions:
1. AUDIT PHASE — Read all of the following files:
   - features/critic_tool.md (what the Critic does; dual-gate spec)
   - features/policy_critic.md (invariants and configuration policy)
   - instructions/HOW_WE_WORK_BASE.md Section 8 (Critic-Driven Coordination)
   - instructions/ARCHITECT_BASE.md (Critic startup mentions)
   - instructions/BUILDER_BASE.md (Critic startup mentions)
   - instructions/QA_BASE.md (Critic mandate sections)

2. CHECK FOR INCONSISTENCIES:
   - Terminology: Does every file use 'coordination engine' (not 'quality gate')? The term 'Critic Quality Gate' is deprecated; flag any occurrences.
   - Routing rules: Are BUG/DISCOVERY/INTENT_DRIFT/SPEC_DISPUTE routing rules consistent across policy_critic.md, HOW_WE_WORK_BASE.md Section 7.5, and QA_BASE.md? Flag any divergence.
   - Role status values: Are builder and QA status enumerations (DONE/FAIL/TODO/INFEASIBLE vs CLEAN/N/A/TODO/DISPUTED/FAIL) consistent across all files?
   - Deprecated config: Is critic_gate_blocking described as a no-op everywhere it appears? Flag any file that still treats it as active.
   - Startup mandate: Does every role instruction file mandate running tools/cdd/status.sh at session start? Flag any omissions.
   - CLI-only contract: Does every file that mentions the agent interface specify CLI (never HTTP)? Flag any contradictions.

3. REPORT: Present a table of findings (CRITICAL / WARNING / OK) for each check.
   - CRITICAL inconsistencies: Halt. Do not proceed to README update. Report which files must be fixed before this step can complete.
   - WARNING inconsistencies: Present to user. Proceed only if user confirms.
   - All OK: Proceed to step 4.

4. README UPDATE — Write or update the '## The Critic' section in README.md.
   - Section placement: immediately after '## The Agents' section, before '## Setup & Configuration'.
   - If the section already exists, diff against the current content and update only what has drifted; do not discard customized wording without confirmation.
   - Content to write:
     a. What the Critic is (coordination engine, not a badge)
     b. Dual-gate validation (Spec Gate + Implementation Gate) — one sentence each
     c. Supplementary audits — bulleted list (User Testing, Builder Decisions, Visual Spec, Untracked Files)
     d. Role-specific action items — brief table or bulleted list
     e. CDD vs. Critic (one-line contrast)
     f. CLI invocation: tools/cdd/status.sh (auto-runs Critic), tools/critic/run.sh

5. Commit README.md with message: 'docs(readme): update Role of the Critic section'.

steps.description: Audits `.purlin/` override files for instructions that are framework-level rules and should be promoted to base instruction files. Complements `purlin.instruction_audit` (which checks for contradictions) by catching rules that accidentally landed in overrides when they belong in the base layer. Purlin-specific: only meaningful in the framework repository where base files are editable.
steps.agent_instructions:
1. READ ALL FOUR OVERRIDE/BASE PAIRS:
   - .purlin/HOW_WE_WORK_OVERRIDES.md  ↔  instructions/HOW_WE_WORK_BASE.md
   - .purlin/ARCHITECT_OVERRIDES.md     ↔  instructions/ARCHITECT_BASE.md
   - .purlin/BUILDER_OVERRIDES.md       ↔  instructions/BUILDER_BASE.md
   - .purlin/QA_OVERRIDES.md            ↔  instructions/QA_BASE.md

2. EXTRACT AND NUMBER RULES: For each override file, identify every discrete
   rule or section. Assign sequential numbers across ALL files (e.g.,
   HOW_WE_WORK rules 1-3, ARCHITECT rules 4-7, BUILDER rules 8-9, etc.).

3. CLASSIFY EACH RULE:
   - PROMOTE: Universal contracts (submodule safety, agent behavior patterns,
     tool interaction protocols) that apply to ALL Purlin consumer projects.
   - PROMOTE: Rules about how agents handle submodule contexts in general.
   - KEEP: Rules specific to THIS repository (dev/ folder conventions, sample
     config sync prompts, framework documentation processes, Purlin CI/CD).
   - KEEP: Rules referencing purlin-config-sample/, framework doc builds, or
     dev/ scripts.

4. PRESENT THE AUDIT TABLE grouped by override file:

   | # | Override File | Rule Summary | Rec. | Rationale |
   |---|---|---|---|---|

   Ask: "Which rules should be promoted? Enter numbers (e.g., 1,3,7) or
   'none' to skip."

5. FOR EACH APPROVED PROMOTION:
   a. Read the target base file in full.
   b. CONFLICT CHECK: Search for existing sections covering the same concern.
      If found, present the overlap and ask: merge, replace, or skip.
   c. If clean or user confirms: use /pl-edit-base to insert the promoted
      content into the appropriate base file section.
   d. After the base edit is committed, REMOVE the promoted rule from the
      override file and commit.

6. POST-PROMOTION VALIDATION: Run /pl-override-edit --scan-only on each modified
   override file. Removing a rule can unmask latent conflicts — catch them here.

7. REPORT: If no promotions needed, report "Override Promotion Audit: CLEAN —
   all override rules are project-specific." If promotions occurred, report
   summary: "N rules promoted, M overrides modified, conflict check CLEAN."

steps.description: Pushes the release commits and tags to the public GitHub repository (github remote). This is the public distribution point for the Purlin framework.
steps.agent_instructions:
1. Verify the `github` remote exists by running `git remote get-url github`. If it does not exist, halt and report that the github remote is not configured.
2. Get the URL: run `git remote get-url github` and display it.
3. Confirm with the user: 'Push `main` and tags to `github` (`<url>`)?' Do not proceed without confirmation.
4. Fetch from github remote and check that `github/main` is not ahead of local main. If ahead, halt and report the divergence.
5. Execute: `git push github main && git push github --tags`.
6. If force-push would be required, warn the user and do not proceed without explicit confirmation.
7. Report the push result including branches and tags updated.

steps.description: Purlin-repository-specific pre-push audit that verifies all framework scripts, tools, and instruction files are safe for submodule deployment. Checks for known failure modes when tools/ lives inside a git submodule.
steps.agent_instructions:
1. AUDIT SCOPE — Examine all Python tools under tools/ (critic.py, serve.py, and any other
   .py files), all shell scripts under tools/ (status.sh, start.sh, run.sh, init.sh),
   and base instruction files (instructions/*.md).

2. CHECK 1 — PURLIN_PROJECT_ROOT env var (contract §2.11): Does each Python tool check
   os.environ.get('PURLIN_PROJECT_ROOT') before path climbing? Does each shell script check
   $PURLIN_PROJECT_ROOT before relative-path fallback? Do the launcher templates in
   init.sh export PURLIN_PROJECT_ROOT? CRITICAL if any are missing.

3. CHECK 2 — Climbing priority (contract §2.11): Do Python tools try the FURTHER path first
   (../../../.purlin/config.json — submodule layout) BEFORE the nearer path
   (../../.purlin/config.json — standalone layout) when PURLIN_PROJECT_ROOT is not set?
   CRITICAL if nearer-first order is found.

4. CHECK 3 — Artifact write locations (contract §2.12): Search for any writes to paths under
   tools/ for .log, .pid, .json, or cache output. Permitted: .purlin/runtime/ and
   .purlin/cache/. CRITICAL if tools/ writes are found.

5. CHECK 4 — Config JSON safety (contract §2.13): Search Python tools for bare json.load()
   or json.loads() on config files without try/except. WARNING if unguarded calls found.

6. CHECK 5 — CWD-relative paths (contract §2.14): Search Python tools for bare os.getcwd()
   or open('features/...') without a project root variable. Search shell scripts for bare
   relative paths to project-level directories. CRITICAL if found.

7. CHECK 6 — sed JSON safety (contract §2.10): Find all sed commands on .json files in shell
   scripts. Verify each is followed by a python3 json.load validation step. Check the regex
   does not risk stripping structural characters. CRITICAL if structural risk; WARNING if no
   validation follows.

8. CHECK 7 — Instruction file path awareness: Scan instruction files for direct 'tools/'
   references lacking tools_root context. WARNING if unqualified reference found.

9. REPORT — Output findings table (Category | File | Finding | Severity).
   HALT if any CRITICAL. Confirm with user if any WARNING. Report CLEAN if all pass.

steps.description: Synchronizes project documentation from docs/ to Confluence pages under the PRODENG space. Uses Atlassian MCP for page operations and a Python script for image attachment uploads.
steps.agent_instructions:
Phase 0 — Prerequisites and First-Time Setup:
1. Check Confluence MCP availability: search for Atlassian MCP tools (e.g., use ToolSearch for 'atlassian' tools).
   a. If Atlassian MCP tools ARE available in the current session: proceed to step 2.
   b. If Atlassian MCP tools are NOT available:
      i. Check if `.mcp.json` in the project root already contains an 'atlassian' entry (read the file).
      ii. If NOT configured: run `claude mcp add --transport http --scope project atlassian https://mcp.atlassian.com/v2/mcp` to configure it automatically.
      iii. Whether newly configured or already configured, the MCP is not loaded in this session. Inform the user: 'Atlassian MCP is now configured but MCP servers load at session start. Please restart Claude Code and re-run /pl-release-run 8.' HALT. Do NOT ask the user to run any commands themselves.
2. Check API token: read `.purlin/runtime/confluence/credentials.json`.
   a. If present with 'email' and 'api_token' keys: proceed to step 3.
   b. If missing: run `mkdir -p .purlin/runtime/confluence/`, then ask the user for their Atlassian email and API token (direct them to https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens to create one with label 'purlin-docs-sync'). Write the credentials file automatically. Verify with a test API call. Halt until working.
3. Verify Confluence access: use the Atlassian MCP to list spaces and confirm the PRODENG space is accessible. The MCP handles OAuth automatically — a browser window will open if needed. Halt if PRODENG space is not found.
4. If all prerequisites pass: proceed to Phase 1.

Phase 1 — Doc Freshness Review:
1. Read each docs/**/*.md file.
2. Cross-reference against current implementation in tools/, instructions/, and features/.
3. Check for: stale command references, outdated workflow descriptions, removed/renamed concepts.
4. Update stale content and commit: docs: update <filename> for current implementation.
5. If no updates needed, confirm docs are current.

Phase 2 — Image Upload:
1. Scan all markdown files for ![alt](path) image references.
2. If images found: determine target page ID for each image (the child page it belongs to), run python3 dev/confluence_upload_images.py --page-id <id> --files <paths>, collect URL mapping.
3. If no images: skip to Phase 3.

Phase 3 — Confluence Page Sync:
1. Search for existing child pages under parent page 4011458562.
2. For each subdirectory in docs/: derive section page title (directory name to title-case; reference maps to Technical Reference), find or create section page as child of parent.
3. For each *.md file: derive page title from filename (hyphens to spaces, title-case), read markdown content, replace local image paths with Confluence URLs if applicable, create or update child page under section page via MCP.
4. NEVER delete pages without a local counterpart.

Phase 4 — Completion Report:
Print a change-focused summary with sections: Local updates (CHANGED/OK), Images (UPLOADED/SKIPPED), Confluence sync (CREATED/UPDATED), page count and parent URL, and Recommendations (only if gaps detected).
